headline: MARA Education Budget RM5.2B Serves 3% of Students Exclusively
context: MARA's education budget of RM5.2B in 2025 serves exclusively Bumiputera students — approximately 3% of total student population — at a per-student cost 8 times the national average.

cards.big: MARA spends RM5.2B annually on education for 3% of Malaysia's students.
cards.sub: Per-student cost: RM42,000. National average: RM5,200. The ratio is 8:1.

cards.big: MARA Junior Science Colleges produce 4% of national SPM candidates.
cards.sub: Their results are above average but not proportional to 8x spending — suggesting diminishing returns.

cards.big: MARA overseas scholarships cost RM180,000 per student per year.
cards.sub: Equivalent local university education costs RM12,000. The ROI gap is 15:1.

cards.big: 60% of MARA scholarship recipients are from T20 Bumiputera households.
cards.sub: The programme disproportionately benefits those who least need assistance.

cards.big: RM5.2B for 3% of students is not affirmative action. It is elite education funded by public money.
MARA's mission is valid. Its targeting is not. Means-testing and domestic study options could serve 5 times more B40 Bumiputera students at the same cost.
